item.title: Australia's gas reservation scheme 'creates more questions than answers'
item.description: Critics' concerns include how national scheme will operate alongside state-level policies and uncertainty over long-term domestic market balance

item.title: Woodside mulls pre-empting Inpex's gas megaproject deal
item.description: Japanese company had agreed to purchase PetroChina's 10.67% interest in Browse — Australia's largest unexploited offshore gas field

item.title: Leading joint venture wins major Malaysia FPSO prize
item.description: Project lies off the coast of Sabah, East Malaysia, and includes the Kikeh, Siakap North and Petai fields

item.title: Sinopec unveils China's first huge ultra-deep shale gas field
item.description: Field holds proven reserves of more than 235 billion cubic metres of shale gas

item.title: Pipeline reforms boost Australia’s CCS industry
item.description: Northern Territory passes landmark legislation allowing CO2 to flow through existing infrastructure

item.title: US extends sanctions waiver on Russian oil
item.description: Latest OFAC notice allows Russian oil on water to be sold until 17 June

item.title: US government official urges 'healthy dialogue' on new Venezuela deals
item.description: Department of Energy official urges delegates to network and cut deals to open Venezuela's oil and gas sector

item.title: Kazakh oilfield maintenance set to hit supplies to Europe
item.description: Kashagan oilfield in Caspian will shut production in June despite high energy prices fuelled by the Iran war

item.title: YPF plans $25 billion investment to boost Vaca Muerta oil production
item.description: Programme under RIGI initiative will focus on five blocks in the prolific shale play

item.title: Commercial oil inventories falling ‘rapidly’ and only have weeks left: IEA chief
item.description: Fatih Birol warns reserves ‘are not endless’ as energy markets continue recovery from US-Iran war

item.title: TotalEnergies' critical new FSO vessel arrives in Chinese yard
item.description: Adiana will replace ageing Unity FSO in Nigerian waters

item.title: US major agrees to gas supply deal for LNG development
item.description: ConocoPhillips to provide gas for Glenfarne’s Alaska LNG

item.title: Turkish Petroleum's latest drillship marks debut operation in Black Sea
item.description: Latest addition to six-strong fleet arrived at Sakarya gas field

item.title: Santos and Repsol rev up Alaskan oilfield
item.description: Pikka in Alaska comes on stream

item.title: The all-seeing project that could help unlock Japanese floating wind
item.description: Ocergy's design is already at the heart of two key floating wind projects in Europe and this California company with a distinctly French flavour is about to achieve an unusual entry into the Japanese market

item.title: Two huge UK offshore wind projects win planning consent
item.description: Offshore wind farm being developed in two phases in North Sea by RWE and Masdar could power 3 million UK homes annually

item.title: VIDEO: Orsted installs first monopile at UK North Sea wind farm
item.description: Watch Upstream's sister title Recharge's video of the first of 197 XXL monopiles being installed on Orsted’s Hornsea 3 offshore wind farm in the UK North Sea. The XXL monopiles are the largest used by Orsted on any of its European wind farms to date and each will be topped by a 15 megawatt turbine. They were installed using Cadeler’s Wind Ally installation vessel. (Video credit: ORSTED)

item.title: OMV starts up Austria gas field to help secure winter supplies
item.description: OMV will produce gas at Wittau field in Lower Austria from depth of more than 5000 metres

item.title: UK Conservative Party to call for vote on North Sea production
item.description: Conservative Party argues UK North Sea production key to domestic energy security

item.title: End in sight for teething troubles on BW Offshore FPSO
item.description: Norwegian contractor also busy bidding for new contracts in the Americas

item.title: Strait of Hormuz expected to reopen ‘sometime this summer at latest’, US energy secretary says
item.description: Chris Wright tours Golden Pass as LNG carriers line up to exit Middle East Gulf

item.title: Adnoc accelerates plan for pipeline bypassing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: New onshore pipeline aims to boost Abu Dhabi's oil export capacity to almost 3.3 million bpd via the port of Fujairah

item.title: Geology vs policy: How North Sea gas could bolster UK energy security
item.description: Analysts question government claims that North Sea gas production will not improve energy security or impact bills

item.title: Malaysian rig owner secures landmark contract with Hibiscus
item.description: Velesto Energy to deploy mystery owner’s rig instead of unit from its own fleet

item.title: Top contractors competing for coveted offshore prize at world's largest gas field
item.description: QatarEnergy LNG is expected to soon select its preferred contractor for front-end engineering and design work on the COMP6 project

item.title: Australian state proposes to lift fracking moratorium
item.description: South Australia is latest jurisdiction eyeing more exploration amid looming threat of gas supply shortages

item.title: End of the legal road for Petronas
item.description: Historical dispute with Kingtime relates to patent infringement for MOPU, $133 million damages claimed

item.title: Bid process gathers pace for new Asian offshore gas development
item.description: EPCM project involves integrated development of at least three blocks in Tapti-Daman sector of Mumbai Offshore basin

item.title: Equus Energy buoyant on prospect of exploiting namesake giant field
item.description: Equus gas condensate field development will see first-ever deployment of Sevan cylindrical FPSO in Australian waters

item.title: Canada set to confirm deal for new oil export pipeline to west coast
item.description: Ottawa and Alberta are inking deal to export more Canadian crude to Asian customers via British Columbia port
